definition: if a road, river, etc bends, it changes direction in the shape of a curve. A bend is a curve in a road, river, etc
example: a sharp bend in the road/river
the river bends northeast towards the estuary

definition: brakes are the equipment in a vehicle that you use for stopping or slowing down. If you brake, you stop or slow down the vehicle by using the brakes
example: I had to brake sharply to avoid crashing into the back of her car
I saw a small boy run out so I slammed on the brakes

definition: a bump is a raised, uneven part on a surface. If a journey is bumpy, it is uncomfortable because the vehicle you are in moves up and down a lot on the surface you are travelling along
example: the road was really bumpy
we hit a bump in the road

definition: if you calm down, you become less excited, angry, or upset. If you calm someone down, you make them feel less excited, angry, or upset
example: it took me a few minutes to calm down

definition: if a vehicle carries someone or something, it takes them there. A carriage is one of the separate sections of a train that carries passengers. The American word is car
example: they sat in the first-class carriage
a train carrying over 200 passengers has crashed in southern Spain

definition: if you check in at a hotel or airport, you give your personal details and show tickets etc so that you can use the hotel or travel on the flight
example: we’ll go and check in at our hotel first
there was a huge queue at the check-in desk
there was a huge queue at the check-in desk

definition: the deck of a boat is the outside part where you can walk. A deck is also one of the different levels or areas on a boat, plane, or bus
example: it was too windy to go out on deck

definition: a flat tyre does not have enough air in it
example: I think you’ve got a flat tyre
twenty miles after we set off, I got a flat

definition: if you go through a difficult or unpleasant situation, you experience it
example: I don’t want to go through that again

definition: if you hang around, you stay somewhere waiting or doing nothing
example: they had to hang around for a while until the next train

definition: a harbour is an area of sea next to the land where it is safe for boats to stay, and where people can get on and off boats
example: it’s a coastal town with a pretty fishing harbour

definition: a line is a part of a railway system, or the metal track that trains travel along
example: there are engineering works on the London to Brighton line

definition: if you overtake another vehicle when you are driving, you go past it because you are moving faster
example: it’s too dangerous to overtake here

definition: at a railway station, the platforms are the areas next to the track, where passengers get on and off trains
example: the 9.23 to Newcastle will depart from platform 3

definition: if the sea is rough, there are a lot of waves on the surface because the weather is bad
example: the ferry was delayed because of rough seas

definition: if you set off, you begin a journey
example: they set off early the next day

definition: if you slip, your foot moves accidentally and you lose your balance or fall. A surface that is slippery is very smooth or wet and difficult to walk on, so it could make you slip
example: watch out, the ground is really slippery over there
It’s muddy over there - be careful you don’t slip

definition: if a journey across the sea is smooth, the boat you are travelling in does not move about much because there are not many waves on the surface of the sea
example: we had a smooth crossing and I didn’t feel ill at all

definition: something that is terrifying makes you feel extremely frightened
example: it was a terrifying experience that I’d rather forget
the cave was terrifyingly dark
the cave was terrifyingly dark
the shootings terrified the neighbourhood

definition: a tight bend is a bend in a road, track, etc which curves a lot and is difficult to drive around
example: the two vehicles collided on a tight bend

definition: a tyre is a thick rubber cover which fits around the wheel of a car, bicycle, or other vehicle
example: we got a flat tyre on the motorway

definition: asylum is the right to stay in a country. It is given by a government to someone who is unable to return to their own country for political reasons
example: over 500 refugees were granted asylum

definition: if something or someone collapses, they suddenly fall down. If a system collapses, it suddenly fails or comes to an end
example: if the government hadn’t helped the banks, the economy would have collapsed
various factors contributed to the collapse of the economy
collocations: be on the brink/ point/verge of collapse

definition: if something is well connected, it has good links with other things
example: refugees are often well connected, and may have friends and families in countries all over the world

definition: something to/with something
example: there are good connections between the capital and other towns

definition: considerable means large in amount or degree
example: renovation of the building is likely to cost a considerable amount of money
their room was considerably larger than ours

definition: if you make a contribution to something, you do something to help make it successful or to achieve it contribution
example: this kind of industry makes a significant contribution to the national economy
Mr Fellowes didn’t really contribute much to the discussion

definition: someone who is critical gives their opinion when they think something is wrong or bad
example: the writer is critical of the way immigration is often discussed
constructive criticism
criticise someone for (doing) something

definition: a detention centre is a place like a prison for people who don’t have permission to be in a country. They are kept there while the authorities decide whether to allow them to stay
example: Hussain was then kept in a government detention centre for another nine months before finally being allowed to stay in the country
translation: центр содержания мигрантов, центр временного содержания

definition: if someone is fit, they are healthy and can do physical exercise without becoming tired
example: our team might have won if all our players had been fit
he needs to work on his fitness if he’s going to win

definition: if you flee a dangerous place or situation, you escape from it quickly
example: thousands of refugees are fleeing across the border to safety

definition: a gang is a group of people who spend a lot of time together, often for criminal reasons
example: they were operating as an organised gang

definition: if you generate something, you produce it or cause it to develop
example: these small businesses never generate much money
electricity generation

definition: a hazard is something that could be dangerous or cause damage. If something is hazardous, it could be dangerous for a person’s health or safety
example: he had survived in some of the most hazardous waters on Earth
smoking is a well-known health hazard

definition: someone who is mature has a fully developed personality and behaves in a sensible way
example: Anna was much more mature than the other girls in the group

definition: if someone matures, their personality becomes more fully developed and they behave in a sensible way
example: it’s not just individuals that mature and grow because of immigration, but nations as well
John had acquired a sudden remarkable maturity
Pablo can be so annoying at times, he’s very immature

definition: an ordeal is a very difficult and unpleasant experience
example: the two women suffered a terrifying ordeal

definition: a refugee is someone who has left their country because of war or their political or religious beliefs, or because of an event such as an earthquake, flood, etc
example: the troubles have caused thousands to flock to refugee camps

definition: if you seek something, you try to get it. A seeker is someone who is trying to find or get something
example: he’s always been a publicity seeker
if these symptoms persist, then seek medical advice

definition: a skill is an ability to do something. A skill set is the range of things that a person can do well
example: having a broad skill set has provided a lot of opportunities

definition: if you squeeze a person or thing somewhere, or if they squeeze somewhere, they manage to move into a small space
example: we managed to squeeze everything into one bag
it was a bit of a squeeze, but we all managed to find a seat

definition: a target is an object that you try to hit in a game or sport. If you target someone or something, you try or intend to attack them
example: as a member of an ethnic minority he was often targeted and treated badly
tourists have become targets for terrorist attacks

definition: if you are allowed something, or if you are allowed to do something, you are given permission to have or do it. An allowance is an amount of something that you are officially allowed to have by a particular set of rules
example: you need to be careful that you don’t exceed the baggage allowance of 20 kilos
smoking is not allowed in the auditorium

definition: if you assume that something is true, you believe it is true even though you do not have proof
example: I assume that it’s hot in Spain at the moment
I’m sorry, but that’s a completely false assumption

definition: if you bite something, you use your teeth to cut into it or make a hole in it. A bite is an injury or mark on your skin where an insect or other animal has bitten you
example: he had a nasty bite on his leg
does Jack’s rabbit bite?

definition: if you blame someone or something, you say that they have caused a problem for something
example: I blame the media for all this fuss
he doesn’t deserve the blame for what happened

definition: if someone is careful, they think about what they are doing so that they avoid problems or mistakes
example: his success is down to careful planning
read the instructions carefully
he was so careless he left his wallet at home
he carelessly dropped his briefcase on the bed

definition: conditions are the things that must be true or be done as part of an agreement, law, or contract
example: you have to meet strict financial conditions in order to qualify

definition: excess means more than is usual or needed
example: drain off any excess fat and add water
tests revealed an excess of alcohol in the victim’s blood
the restaurant was lovely but the prices were a bit excessive

definition: something that is fascinating is very interesting
example: I found her story absolutely fascinating
geology is a subject that has always fascinated me
geology is a subject that has always fascinated me
Eden is a fascinatingly complex character

definition: if someone or something is filthy, they are very dirty
example: I must change this tablecloth, it’s filthy
everything was covered in filth

definition: if you hang something somewhere, you put it so that it is held at the top, and the bottom doesn’t touch the ground
example: you can hang your coats over there
I washed my clothes and hung them up to dry

definition: if you do something in advance, you do it before a particular time or event in the future
example: you can reserve seats in advance

definition: if a place or object is packed, it is full of people or things
example: every museum was packed with tourists

definition: a rash is an area of red spots that you get on your skin if you eat or come into contact with something that makes your body sick
example: I’ve got a rash on my legs

definition: if someone or something is soaked, they are extremely wet
example: take an umbrella or you’ll get soaked
last night’s heavy rain soaked the tent and all our stuff
